etymology.description: 免(免) (miǎn, "to exempt") + 費(費) (fèi, "cost") = to exempt from cost -> free of charge
etymology.interpretation: 免(免) (miǎn, "to exempt") involves the idea of releasing or sparing someone from an obligation, leading to the concept of exemption. 費(費) (fèi, "cost") refers to a required expenditure or amount needing to be paid. The notion of sparing someone from having to pay the cost directly connects to the idea of being 'free of charge'. This makes use of the concept of alleviating a financial burden altogether.

parts.english: to exempt; to spare; to excuse; to dismiss

examples.chinese: 这家博物馆每周日免费开放(這家博物館每週日免費開放)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā bówùguǎn měi zhōurì miǎnfèi kāifàng.
examples.english: This museum is free of charge on Sundays.

examples.chinese: 您可以免费领取一份礼物(您可以免費領取一份禮物)。
examples.pinyin: Nín kěyǐ miǎnfèi lǐngqǔ yī fèn lǐwù.
examples.english: You can receive a gift for free.

examples.chinese: 他们提供免费的咖啡和茶(他們提供免費的咖啡和茶)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen tígōng miǎnfèi de kāfēi hé chá.
examples.english: They offer free coffee and tea.
